mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during months with rising PAYEMS (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate of more than 0.25 percentage points within three months signals restrictive monetary policy that tightens financial conditions and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the trigger is active over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below its natural rate and the federal funds rate is simultaneously rising, the interaction of tight labor markets with active monetary tightening compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.5% and FEDFUNDS increasing over prior 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During industrial contraction regimes, two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal deteriorating corporate profitability and weaker S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during triggered periods within industrial contraction regimes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index of more than 0.3 percentage points signals upside inflation surprises that raise discount rates and compress real earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led expansion regime, sustained increases in housing starts signal upstream demand for construction materials, labor income, and mortgage activity that translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than zero (t-test p > 0.05) during triggered periods within housing-led expansions over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls confirm broad-based consumer demand and labor income growth, which jointly drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of joint-increase months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized signals demand deceleration that leads to downward revisions in corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered quarters over any 5-year rolling window.
